title: podman system reset
body:
### Issue Description

After executing `podman system reset` I am seeing avc denied.

I am on fedora coreos latest
cat /etc/os-release
NAME="Fedora Linux"
VERSION="42.20251012.3.0 (CoreOS)"

I have a separate mount /dev/vdb1 on /var/home and after reset I am seeing avc denied

```
type=AVC msg=audit(11/04/25 08:16:45.559:199) : avc:  denied  { search } for  pid=2059 comm=pasta.avx2 name=.local dev="vdb1" ino=10129359 scontext=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:pasta_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:gconf_home_t:s0 tclass=dir permissive=0
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. separate mount for /var/home
2. setsebool -P container_use_devices=true
3. system works for a year
4. today when executed podman system reset, started getting avc denied for dvb1


### Describe the results you received

```
type=AVC msg=audit(11/04/25 08:16:45.559:199) : avc:  denied  { search } for  pid=2059 comm=pasta.avx2 name=.local dev="vdb1" ino=10129359 scontext=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:pasta_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:gconf_home_t:s0 tclass=dir permissive=0
```
After setting permissive, I see some more avc
```
----
type=AVC msg=audit(11/04/25 12:57:45.873:211) : avc:  denied  { write open } for  pid=2043 comm=pasta.avx2 path=/var/home/MYUSER/.local/share/containers/storage/networks/rootless-netns/rootless-netns-conn.pid dev="vdb1" ino=13651478 scontext=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:pasta_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:data_home_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=1
----
type=AVC msg=audit(11/04/25 12:57:45.873:212) : avc:  denied  { read open } for  pid=2043 comm=pasta.avx2 path=/var/home/MYUSER/.local/share/containers/storage/networks/rootless-netns dev="vdb1" ino=13651409 scontext=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:pasta_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:data_home_t:s0 tclass=dir permissive=1
----
type=AVC msg=audit(11/04/25 12:57:45.874:213) : avc:  denied  { getattr } for  pid=2043 comm=pasta.avx2 name=/ dev="vdb1" ino=2 scontext=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:pasta_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=system_u:object_r:fs_t:s0 tclass=filesystem permissive=1
----
type=AVC msg=audit(11/04/25 12:57:45.874:214) : avc:  denied  { watch } for  pid=2043 comm=pasta.avx2 path=/var/home/MYUSER/.local/share/containers/storage/networks/rootless-netns dev="vdb1" ino=13651409 scontext=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:pasta_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:data_home_t:s0 tclass=dir permissive=1
----
type=AVC msg=audit(11/04/25 12:57:48.165:239) : avc:  denied  { associate } for  pid=2337 comm=apache2 name=2 scontext=system_u:object_r:container_t:s0:c161,c369 tcontext=system_u:object_r:proc_t:s0 tclass=filesystem permissive=1
```
 
### Describe the results you expected

no avc

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
>   runRoot: /var/home/MYUSER/.local/share/containers/storage

How did you configure this system? The runroot should be on a tmpfs and by default generally XDG_RUNTIME_DIR or if that is unavailable something under /tmp.

Having the runroot set to the same as graphroot is definitely not something supportable. Do you have a storage.conf set with such an invalid path or where this this path come from?

When setting up this system in early 2024, based on online search I created this storage.conf as `/var` mount is small.

`cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf`

```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage"
graphroot = "$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage"
```

is it safe to change runroot to  `/run/user/$(id -uj)/containers` or it requires system reset?
or better I can just remove the ruiRoot from my storage.conf

> 
> ```
> [storage]
> driver = "overlay"
> runroot = "$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage"
> graphroot = "$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage"
> ```

Beside the runroot this should just be default setting anyway so you should be able to just delete the file I think.

But yes changing settings there does require a podman system reset again.

Removed the storage.conf podman system reset and it works as expected no more avc.
Thank you
